title: Tapping for the Dream House
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the grief and frustration of not living in the home you want
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the persistent longing for a different home: the grief of not being where you planned, the comparisons, the feeling that life is happening somewhere else. Works through the attachment to the future home toward finding something real in the present one.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when longing for a different home is creating a sense that your current life is on hold, or when house-browsing has tipped from pleasure into pain.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the dream house that is not yet real. The script works through the grief of the gap between where you live and where you wish you lived, and moves toward something that can be accessed in the actual home.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I don't live in the home I want and it bothers me more than I admit, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I keep browsing for a home I can't have yet and it makes me feel worse about this one, I accept where I am.
Even though part of my life feels like it's on hold until the real house arrives, I am open to finding something good here now.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The grief of the house I can picture but can't get to yet.
Side of Eye: Measuring this home against the ideal one.
Under Eye: The sense that real life begins somewhere else.
Under Nose: The things I won't do here because it isn't permanent.
Chin: The dream house as a place where everything is finally sorted.
Collarbone: Holding the current home at arm's length.
Under Arm: The disappointment I carry about where I ended up.
Top of Head: All the life happening in this home anyway, whether I notice it or not.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: This home is real. My life in it is real.
Side of Eye: I can want more and also appreciate what is here.
Under Eye: The things I am saving for the future house: some of them are possible now.
Under Nose: I release the hold the future house has on the present one.
Chin: I can work toward the home I want and inhabit the home I have.
Collarbone: Something about this home is worth having right now.
Under Arm: I am here. This is where my life is happening.
Top of Head: I can be present in this home while still planning for a different one.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
